label: Arctic
comfortReason: Extreme cold, constant wind, no natural shelter. Everything freezes. Survival depends on preparation and fire.
scoutGuidance: Survival check. Poor: 2-7 | Average: 8-12 | Good: 13+. Ranger or outlander has advantage. Cold weather gear recommended.

nat1: A natural ice hollow, packed snow walls blocking the wind. Someone wintered here before.
A frozen stream bed with high banks on both sides. Perfect windbreak.
A sheltered depression behind a pressure ridge. Invisible from the flats.
poor: Open tundra. Nothing but flat white in every direction.
A slight dip in the ice. Marginal cover.
Packed snow, at least. Flat enough to sleep on.
average: A cluster of ice boulders providing some wind cover.
A firm snow bank deep enough to dig into.
A frozen ridge offering partial shelter.
good: A natural snow cave, stable and insulated.
A sheltered ice ravine with high walls.
A wind-carved alcove in a glacier face.
nat20: An abandoned expedition cache shelter, still intact.
A geothermal vent warming a sheltered hollow. Steam rises gently.
A deep ice cave, eerily beautiful and completely still.
